shortDescription: Lightweight X (Twitter) company presence — search recent posts, people, and topics using the Twitter API v2. Outbound posting is defined but requires OAuth 2.0 user-context credentials not yet available.

externalAccountsNeeded.description: Twitter API v2 Bearer Token (app-only, from developer.twitter.com)
externalAccountsNeeded.howToObtain: Create an App at developer.twitter.com, copy the Bearer Token from the App Keys page, then tell Helkin: store this Twitter Bearer Token for me.

orchestratorUseCases: search recent X / Twitter posts about a topic, person, or company
monitor mentions and social media signals on X/Twitter
check what people are saying about a topic on X
search for relevant conversations or thought leaders on X

capabilityGroups.shortDescription: Search and monitor X/Twitter content. Outbound posting reserved for future OAuth 2.0 user-context integration.
useWhen: user wants to search X or Twitter for recent content or social signals

tools.name: x_search
tools.description: Search X (Twitter) for recent posts, people, and topics using the Twitter API v2 app-only search. Returns up to 10 recent matching tweets with author, timestamp, and text. Requires TWITTER_BEARER_TOKEN env var set by operator.
useWhen: user wants to search X/Twitter for recent posts or news
user wants to monitor mentions or topics on X
orchestrator needs social media signal for a topic or person
avoidWhen: user wants to post or reply on X — use x_post instead
user needs full historical archive search beyond recent 7-day window
typicalInputs: query: string — Twitter search query (keywords, hashtags, phrases, from: filters, etc.)
tools.returnsSummaryShape: { status, query, count, tweets: [{ id, text, authorId, createdAt }] }

tools.name: x_post
tools.description: Compose and publish a post on X/Twitter via the HelkinSwarm company account. NOTE: Currently returns not-configured — outbound posting requires OAuth 2.0 user-context credentials (not available via app-only Bearer token). Requires explicit owner approval before any post is published.
useWhen: user or orchestrator wants to post a public update to X/Twitter from the company account
avoidWhen: user wants to search X — use x_search instead
DMs — not supported by this skill (requires separate per-user OAuth flow)
typicalInputs: text: string — the post text (max 280 characters for standard accounts)
